sources_exist.note: All seven canonical URL occurrences resolve to accessible intended pages.

figures_match_sources.note: The deterministic dataset objects match exactly, and the cited pages support a 3.35x-4.11x travel-agency EBITDA range and TMC seller asking ranges. They do not support the selected 15% firm adoption, 8-10-year analog, 35% owner-age input, three active PE consolidators, transaction-based exit comp, or stated pricing revenue shares.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Most named-role fractions are bounded and directionally plausible, but inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role[10] treats the full 37.87% unlisted wage tail as administrative back office and 55% automatable with no composition evidence. That single unsupported category contributes 0.208285, 44.6% of ai_replaceable_share. pi_dist and pi_moat also rely on uncited exact survey and commission-tier claims, while t50=6 rests on unsupported adoption and analog inputs.

rubric_consistent.note: The record recognizes heterogeneity, supplier-commission capture, direct-booking bypass, and the PRESSURED terminal class. However, source coverage and comparable-population requirements are not met. The assigned BORDERLINE flag is unresolved: |S - pass cut 3.00| = 0.0719, and the unsupported V, C, A, B, and M inputs are each large enough to change the cut outcome.

cross_checks_honest.note: The four mandatory cross-checks are present and the cost-cutting versus product-replacement distinction is candid. Their exact adoption, traveler-AI, billing-mix, and moat premises are not auditable from the record's cited pages, so the borderline conclusion cannot be accepted.

source_audits.note: The opinion article says strategics, PE, family offices, and financial institutions are buyers, and discusses Direct Travel's funded technology strategy. It neither names nor counts three PE-backed platforms actively making $1M-$10M EBITDA travel-agency add-ons in the prior 24 months, and the acquisitions quoted in the input are not on the page.

The valuation firm's page directly reports an average 3.35x-4.11x travel-agency EBITDA range and gives a 3.97x example. The selected 3.5x lies within the cited range, though the page does not label 3.97x as the median or identify the target-size distribution.

The page reports that 58% of corporate travel buyers across the US, Canada, and Europe say AI has had little or no impact on their programs. It does not survey US 561510 agency operators, provide a material-use share, support 15% of firms, or contain the quoted 86% advisor-awareness figure.

The article supports that TMC sellers were looking for 5x-7x EBITDA and transactional B2B travel-tech sellers for 5x-8x. These are asking ranges in an opinion piece, not disclosed paid transaction multiples or $10M+ EBITDA comparable valuations, so they do not establish a 6.5x exit input.

The technology history describes the emergence of internet booking and OTAs, but contains neither 5% of US airline tickets online in 1998 nor internet reservations exceeding GDS reservations in 2006. It therefore does not support the stated 8-10-year diffusion interval.

Travel Weekly reports that 57% of surveyed advisors are older than 55 and that 55% identify as owners or managers. It does not cross-tabulate those facts, isolate owners, provide an age-60 threshold, or support a 35% 60+ share for target-band firm owners.

The guide supports supplier commissions, roughly 10% hotel and boutique-tour commissions, tiering, host/consortium leverage, 0-5% domestic air and 10-22% international air, plus the existence of service fees. It does not support 70-80% of net revenue from commissions, 20-30% from fees, cruise 10-16%, hotel 8-15%, preferred 15-20%, $40-$60 ticket fees, over-half fee adoption, or 42% higher income.

reasons: inputs.current_adoption_pct.url measures corporate travel buyers across multiple countries, not US 561510 agency operators, and contains no 15% material-use rate. Re-run with an operator-side agency survey or label a bounded extrapolation as ESTIMATE before deriving inputs.t50_years.

inputs.historical_analogs.url lacks both quoted endpoint figures. Add primary dated online-booking penetration data on a consistent denominator, then derive inputs.t50_years from the resulting diffusion interval.

inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role[10] assigns all unlisted roles a 55% back-office automation rate without identifying their occupations. Obtain the full occupation wage distribution or create separately evidenced residual buckets; this category alone contributes 0.208285 and materially affects the borderline score.

inputs.pi_dist and inputs.pi_moat cite only non-URL source names while relying on exact IC portability, commission-tier, traveler-AI, and duty-of-care claims. Add accessible direct sources for client ownership, contract retention, accreditation/consortia barriers, and consumer bypass before retaining C.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url reports advisor age over 55, not firm-owner age 60+. Supply an owner-specific age distribution or mark an evidence-bounded estimate, then recompute the B input chain.

inputs.active_consolidators.url does not establish three active PE-backed platforms in the target band. Supply a named platform/add-on ledger for the prior 24 months or correct the estimate and its quality.

inputs.exit_mult.url gives seller asking ranges rather than paid transaction or comparable-company multiples. Replace it with disclosed $10M+ EBITDA platform evidence and state gross-versus-net revenue comparability.

inputs.pricing_structure.url does not support the revenue shares or most quoted rates. Use the linked HAR research report and primary supplier/fee schedules to establish the billing mix, then revisit cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation.

The assigned BORDERLINE flag (|S - pass cut 3.00| = 0.0719) is unresolved because plausible corrections to the unsupported role-tail, adoption, owner-age, capture, consolidator, or exit assumptions can move S across the frozen pass cut. Re-run only after replacing those score-affecting evidence gaps.
